headline: Claude's got 1M context window now (for real), Gemini 3.1 Pro slapping on price, and a whole lotta model drama this week

picks.name: Claude Opus 4.6
picks.tagline: 1M context window + 128K output at standard pricing
picks.summary: Claude just went full beast mode with a 1M token context window (beta), 128K output tokens, and agentic capabilities that actually work. Literally shipped Agent Teams and adaptive thinking without making you pay extra.
picks.bestFor: Long-form reasoning, document analysis, complex coding tasks

picks.name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
picks.tagline: Best price-to-performance ratio with tiered thinking levels
picks.summary: Google dropped Gemini 3.1 Pro with insane ARC-AGI scores (77.1%) at $2/$12 pricing. Low/Medium/High thinking levels let you trade cost vs quality per task. Honestly might be the smartest play for devs watching their bill rn.
picks.bestFor: Cost-conscious dev shops, reasoning tasks, multi-language work

picks.name: Viktor by Zeta Labs
picks.tagline: AI agent that lives in Slack and proactively suggests automations
picks.summary: This Slack agent actually observes how your team works and chimes in with automation ideas you didn't even think of. Built from the Claude Code team, it's lowkey the future of workflow automation—no more context switching.
picks.bestFor: Teams drowning in manual workflows, anyone who lives in Slack

picks.name: Windsurf (IDE)
picks.tagline: Arena Mode lets models compete side-by-side for your coding
picks.summary: Windsurf added Arena Mode so you can pit Claude vs GPT vs other models against each other on your code. Plan Mode now handles smarter task planning before generation. It's like having a team of devs fighting over whose solution is best.
picks.tryIt: Download free IDE with Claude promo pricing
picks.bestFor: Engineers comparing models, anyone building with multi-model strategy

watchNext.title: OpenAI's $110B funding round + Anthropic supply chain drama
watchNext.why: The US government literally labeled Anthropic a supply chain risk over Claude's military restrictions, while OpenAI supposedly reached an 'agreement' with the Department of War. This is reshaping who gets access to what models fr

watchNext.title: 🎙️ The Next Wave: Claude 4.6 vs Gemini 3.1 Pro breakdown
watchNext.why: Matt Wolfe and team dissect the explosion of new models this week and whether agents are finally about to eat GPT-5's lunch. Actually useful comparison of real pricing and use cases.
